message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="purple" summary="Step 1 implementation complete, awaiting review">
Team, I've completed the initial implementation for Step 1: Navigation + Dashboard Skeleton. Here's what was built:

**New files:**
- `src/routes/dashboard.rs` -- Dashboard route handler with current_page tracking
- `src/routes/gtd_placeholders.rs` -- Placeholder handlers for /inbox, /next-actions, /projects, /waiting-for, /someday-maybe, /review
- `templates/dashboard.html` -- Dashboard page with summary stat placeholders (dl/dt/dd)
- `templates/gtd_nav.html` -- Shared GTD nav partial (nav links + quick capture + sign out)
- `templates/gtd_placeholder.html` -- Reusable placeholder template for all GTD pages
- `tests/api/dashboard.rs` -- 8 integration tests for dashboard route
- `tests/api/gtd_placeholders.rs` -- 7 integration tests for all GTD placeholder pages + auth redirect

**Modified files:**
- `templates/base.html` -- Added `hx-boost="true"` on body element
- `templates/login.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on login form (auth forms need full page reload)
- `templates/register.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on register form
- `templates/todos.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on logout form
- `src/routes/index.rs` -- Authenticated users redirect to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/routes/login.rs` -- Post-login redirects to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/routes/register.rs` -- Post-registration redirects to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/startup.rs` -- Added all GTD routes
- `src/routes/mod.rs` -- Exported new modules
- `static/css/main.css` -- Container max-width 40rem->44rem, GTD nav styles, quick capture, dashboard stats, placeholder page styles
- `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` -- Updated for /dashboard redirect, added axe-core a11y scan of dashboard page
- `tests/api/index.rs` -- Updated redirect expectation to /dashboard
- `tests/api/login.rs` -- Updated redirect expectation to /dashboard

**Key decisions:**
1. Header nav with wrapping flexbox, single `<nav aria-label="GTD lists">` landmark
2. `aria-current="page"` with bottom border accent + semibold for active state
3. Quick capture input in header (form posts to /inbox, but POST handler is step 2)
4. `hx-boost="true"` on body for SPA-like navigation between pages
5. Auth forms (login/register/logout) opt out of hx-boost with `hx-boost="false"` so session cookies work properly with full page reloads
6. Dashboard uses `<dl>` for summary stats (semantic label/value pairs)
7. Container max-width bumped from 40rem to 44rem for the wider nav

**Test results:**
- 82 Rust tests pass (was 74 -- +8 new tests)
- 7 Playwright e2e tests pass (was 6 -- +1 new axe-core dashboard a11y test)
- cargo fmt, clippy clean
- SQLx offline data prepared

Please review and provide your feedback. I'm waiting for 9/9 consensus before committing.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Now let me check the routes registration and the tests.

content.content:
1→use crate::helpers::spawn_app; 2→ 3→/// Helper: creates a reqwest client with cookie store and no redirect following. 4→fn authenticated_client() -> reqwest::Client { 5→ reqwest::Client::builder() 6→ .cookie_store(true) 7→ .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()) 8→ .build() 9→ .unwrap() 10→} 11→ 12→/// Helper: register a user and log them in, returning the cookie-jar client. 13→async fn register_and_login(address: &str, email: &str, password: &str) -> reqwest::Client { 14→ let client = authenticated_client(); 15→ 16→ // Register 17→ let resp = client 18→ .post(format!("{address}/register")) 19→ .form(&[ 20→ ("email", email), 21→ ("password", password), 22→ ("password_confirmation", password), 23→ ]) 24→ .send() 25→ .await 26→ .expect("Failed to register"); 27→ assert_eq!(resp.status().as_u16(), 303, "Registration should redirect"); 28→ 29→ // Login 30→ let resp = client 31→ .post(format!("{address}/login")) 32→ .form(&[("email", email), ("password", password)]) 33→ .send() 34→ .await 35→ .expect("Failed to login"); 36→ assert_eq!(resp.status().as_u16(), 303, "Login should redirect"); 37→ 38→ client 39→} 40→ 41→async fn assert_gtd_page( 42→ client: &reqwest::Client, 43→ address: &str, 44→ path: &str, 45→ expected_heading: &str, 46→ expected_current: &str, 47→) { 48→ let response = client 49→ .get(format!("{address}{path}")) 50→ .send() 51→ .await 52→ .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!("Failed to GET {path}")); 53→ 54→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200, "{path} should return 200"); 55→ 56→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 57→ 58→ assert!( 59→ body.contains(&format!("

{expected_heading}

")), 60→ "{path} should have heading '{expected_heading}'" 61→ ); 62→ 63→ // Navigation links present 64→ assert!( 65→ body.contains("href=\"/inbox\""), 66→ "{path} missing link to /inbox" 67→ ); 68→ assert!( 69→ body.contains("href=\"/next-actions\""), 70→ "{path} missing link to /next-actions" 71→ ); 72→ assert!( 73→ body.contains("href=\"/projects\""), 74→ "{path} missing link to /projects" 75→ ); 76→ assert!( 77→ body.contains("href=\"/waiting-for\""), 78→ "{path} missing link to /waiting-for" 79→ ); 80→ assert!( 81→ body.contains("href=\"/someday-maybe\""), 82→ "{path} missing link to /someday-maybe" 83→ ); 84→ assert!( 85→ body.contains("href=\"/review\""), 86→ "{path} missing link to /review" 87→ ); 88→ 89→ // aria-current on the right link 90→ assert!( 91→ body.contains("aria-current=\"page\""), 92→ "{path} should have aria-current=\"page\" on the active nav link" 93→ ); 94→ 95→ // The current link should be the one with aria-current 96→ assert!( 97→ body.contains(&format!("href=\"{path}\" aria-current=\"page\"")) 98→ || body.contains(&format!("href=\"{path}\"\n aria-current=\"page\"")) 99→ || body.contains(expected_current), 100→ "{path} should have aria-current on the correct link" 101→ ); 102→ 103→ // Sign out present 104→ assert!( 105→ body.contains("Sign out"), 106→ "{path} should have sign out button" 107→ ); 108→ 109→ // hx-boost present 110→ assert!( 111→ body.contains("hx-boost=\"true\""), 112→ "{path} should have hx-boost on body" 113→ ); 114→} 115→ 116→#[tokio::test] 117→async fn inbox_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 118→ let app = spawn_app().await; 119→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "inbox@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 120→ assert_gtd_page(&client, &app.address, "/inbox", "Inbox", "inbox").await; 121→} 122→ 123→#[tokio::test] 124→async fn next_actions_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 125→ let app = spawn_app().await; 126→ let client = 127→ register_and_login(&app.address, "nextactions@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 128→ assert_gtd_page( 129→ &client, 130→ &app.address, 131→ "/next-actions", 132→ "Next Actions", 133→ "next_actions", 134→ ) 135→ .await; 136→} 137→ 138→#[tokio::test] 139→async fn projects_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 140→ let app = spawn_app().await; 141→ let client = 142→ register_and_login(&app.address, "projects@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 143→ assert_gtd_page(&client, &app.address, "/projects", "Projects", "projects").await; 144→} 145→ 146→#[tokio::test] 147→async fn waiting_for_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 148→ let app = spawn_app().await; 149→ let client = 150→ register_and_login(&app.address, "waitingfor@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 151→ assert_gtd_page( 152→ &client, 153→ &app.address, 154→ "/waiting-for", 155→ "Waiting For", 156→ "waiting_for", 157→ ) 158→ .await; 159→} 160→ 161→#[tokio::test] 162→async fn someday_maybe_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 163→ let app = spawn_app().await; 164→ let client = register_and_login( 165→ &app.address, 166→ "somedaymaybe@example.com", 167→ "securepassword123", 168→ ) 169→ .await; 170→ assert_gtd_page( 171→ &client, 172→ &app.address, 173→ "/someday-maybe", 174→ "Someday/Maybe", 175→ "someday_maybe", 176→ ) 177→ .await; 178→} 179→ 180→#[tokio::test] 181→async fn review_page_returns_200_with_nav() { 182→ let app = spawn_app().await; 183→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "review@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 184→ assert_gtd_page(&client, &app.address, "/review", "Weekly Review", "review").await; 185→} 186→ 187→#[tokio::test] 188→async fn gtd_pages_redirect_to_login_when_unauthenticated() { 189→ let app = spawn_app().await; 190→ 191→ let client = reqwest::Client::builder() 192→ .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()) 193→ .build() 194→ .unwrap(); 195→ 196→ let pages = [ 197→ "/dashboard", 198→ "/inbox", 199→ "/next-actions", 200→ "/projects", 201→ "/waiting-for", 202→ "/someday-maybe", 203→ "/review", 204→ ]; 205→ 206→ for path in pages { 207→ let response = client 208→ .get(format!("{}{}", &app.address, path)) 209→ .send() 210→ .await 211→ .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!("Failed to GET {path}")); 212→ 213→ assert_eq!( 214→ response.status().as_u16(), 215→ 303, 216→ "{path} should redirect unauthenticated users" 217→ ); 218→ 219→ let location = response 220→ .headers() 221→ .get("location") 222→ .expect("Missing Location header") 223→ .to_str() 224→ .unwrap(); 225→ assert_eq!(location, "/login", "{path} should redirect to /login"); 226→ } 227→} 228→

content.text: Good, I've reviewed all the implementation files. Let me send my domain review to Luca.

input.content:
Luca, domain architect review of Task #11. Overall: solid implementation that establishes the right structure.

**CONSENT** with one suggestion for a future improvement (not blocking).

**What's right:**

1. **Route naming uses GTD ubiquitous language**: `/inbox`, `/next-actions`, `/projects`, `/waiting-for`, `/someday-maybe`, `/review`. These match the domain concepts exactly. Good.

2. **Quick capture form posts to `/inbox`**: Correct. This enforces the domain rule that capture always creates InboxItem. The `action="/inbox"` in `gtd_nav.html` line 14 is the right endpoint.

3. **Dashboard uses `<dl>` for summary stats**: Matches the acceptance criteria and the `DashboardSummary` struct concept. The four stats (Inbox, Next Actions, Stalled Projects, Waiting For) are the right domain counts. Hardcoded to zero is correct for step 1.

4. **`aria-current="page"` driven by `current_page` string comparison**: Works correctly. Each route handler passes the right string value.

5. **Auth protection on all GTD routes**: Every handler extracts `AuthenticatedUser`. The test `gtd_pages_redirect_to_login_when_unauthenticated` verifies all 7 routes. Good coverage.

6. **Placeholder descriptions use GTD language**: "Capture anything on your mind. Process it later." (inbox), "Outcomes that need more than one action step." (projects) -- these are the ubiquitous language. Good.

7. **Existing `/todos` routes preserved**: Coexistence during transition, removed in step 13. Correct.

8. **Test coverage is thorough**: 8 dashboard tests + 7 placeholder tests covering auth redirect, nav links, aria-current, sign out, hx-boost, quick capture. The `assert_gtd_page` helper is a clean pattern for testing consistent nav across all pages.

**One suggestion (non-blocking, future improvement):**

The `current_page: &'static str` field uses string matching in templates. I suggested an `ActivePage` enum for type safety. This isn't blocking for step 1 -- string matching works and is simple. But when we build the real page handlers (steps 2-12), we should consider migrating to an enum so the compiler catches typos. A misspelled `"next_actons"` in a handler would silently fail to highlight the nav link. An `ActivePage::NextActions` variant wouldn't compile with a typo. This can be addressed in a later step when we replace the placeholder handlers.

**No blocking concerns. Ready for consensus.**
input.summary: Domain review CONSENT: routes, naming, auth, quick capture all correct

input.content:
Domain architect review of Luca's Task #11 implementation complete. **CONSENT** -- no blocking concerns.

The implementation correctly uses GTD ubiquitous language for routes, enforces the domain rule that quick capture posts to `/inbox` (InboxItem, not NextAction), uses `<dl>` for semantic summary stats, and has thorough test coverage (15 new tests).

One non-blocking suggestion flagged for future: migrate `current_page: &'static str` to an `ActivePage` enum for type safety when we replace the placeholder handlers.

Ready for consensus collection on Task #11.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation to build a comprehensive summary. 1. **Initial spawn**: I was spawned as Scott Wlaschin (Domain Architect) as a Reviewer for a GTD product discovery task. Marty Cagan is the Driver. 2. **First actions**: Read PROJECT.md, TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md, checked TaskList, read all existing domain code (mod.rs, todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, user.rs, email.rs, password.rs). 3. **Research**: Searched for GTD methodology details, fetched detailed guide from hamberg.no/gtd. 4. **Sent domain analysis to all 8 teammates**: Each tailored to their expertise area. 5. **Received and responded to teammate messages**: - **Luca Palmieri**: Multiple exchanges about schema strategy, domain types, TEXT vs PG enum, ClarifyDecision/ProcessingOutcome separation, completion modeling, unified actions table, migration strategy. - **Kent Beck**: TDD sequencing, "stalled" as domain term, types emerge from tests, captured_at. - **Steve Schoger**: Visual design mapping, stalled project treatment, inbox badge weight, anxiety-reducing design principle. - **Steve Krug**: Wizard vs form for processing, phased delivery, contexts in Phase 1 (he changed his position based on my argument). - **Lea Verou**: CSS component architecture, sticky headers, item modifiers mapped to domain types. - **Carson Gross**: HTMX patterns, transient processing state, context filtering, project redirect during processing. - **Heydon Pickering**: A11y patterns, nav links not tabs, heading hierarchy, processing flow - he corrected my stale context about wizard vs single form. I updated my position to single form with CSS `:has()`. - **Marty Cagan**: Full domain review with CONSENT, answered all 4 open questions, implementation questions about table strategy, action enum vs separate types, project invariant, DoItNow variant. 6. **Key corrections/updates to my positions**: - Migration: Changed from "pending todos -> NextAction" to "pending todos -> inbox_items" (Marty's onboarding argument via Luca). - Processing flow: Changed from wizard/HTMX swaps to single form with CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure (Heydon's correction). - Project invariant: Soft check, not type constraint (agreed with Kent and Luca). 7. **Product discovery documents reviewed**: - docs/gtd-product-discovery.md (NEW) - PROJECT.md (UPDATED) - docs/user-stories.md (UPDATED) - docs/glossary.md (UPDATED) - docs/future-ideas.md (UPDATED) 8. **Task #11 - Implementation Step 1**: Luca became Driver, implemented Navigation + Dashboard skeleton. I reviewed his implementation and gave CONSENT. Let me now organize all the key technical decisions, files, and code patterns. Key decisions reached: - New tables + data migration (not ALTER TABLE) - TEXT discriminator columns, Rust enums as source of truth - Pure domain clarify() function, I/O at service boundary - ClarifyDecision (input) / ProcessingOutcome (output) separation - Completion orthogonal to action type (nullable completed_at) - Contexts as separate entity, @ prefix is display-only - "Stalled project" as review query, not construction constraint - Transient processing state (no partial state in DB) - Separate domain types (NextAction, WaitingForItem) not shared Action enum - Unified actions table for NextAction + WaitingFor - Single form with CSS :has() for processing flow (not wizard) - Quick capture always creates InboxItem, never NextAction - Pending todos migrate to inbox_items (onboarding value) - Default contexts: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @office, @anywhere - Completed actions removed from Next Actions, visible in project view - DoItNow deferred to Could Have Files read during review of Task #11: - src/routes/dashboard.rs - src/routes/gtd_placeholders.rs - templates/dashboard.html - templates/gtd_nav.html - templates/gtd_placeholder.html - src/routes/mod.rs - tests/api/dashboard.rs - tests/api/gtd_placeholders.rs Existing domain files reviewed: - src/domain/mod.rs - src/domain/todo_item.rs - src/domain/todo_title.rs - src/domain/user.rs - src/domain/email.rs - src/domain/password.rs Other files reviewed: - PROJECT.md - TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md - src/startup.rs - docs/gtd-product-discovery.md - docs/user-stories.md - docs/glossary.md - docs/future-ideas.md Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: I was spawned as Scott Wlaschin (Domain Architect), a Reviewer on a 9-person ensemble team building a GTD (Getting Things Done) system evolving from a simple todo list MVP. My primary responsibilities were: - Research GTD from a domain modeling perspective and provide domain analysis to the team - Review Marty Cagan's product discovery documents and provide CONSENT/feedback - Answer open product questions from my domain expertise - Review Luca Palmieri's implementation of Task #11 (Navigation + Dashboard skeleton) - Communicate domain insights tailored to each teammate's expertise area - Operate in read-only mode (Reviewer role - no file modifications) 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **GTD as a state machine**: Items flow through a processing pipeline (Capture -> Clarify -> Organize -> Review -> Engage), not a flat list - **Processing pipeline**: `InboxItem` + `ClarifyDecision` -> `ProcessingOutcome` (pure domain function, atomic operation) - **ClarifyDecision (input) vs ProcessingOutcome (output)**: Separation of user input from domain output - **Transient processing state**: Processing decisions are UI state (hidden form fields), not persisted domain state. Domain operation is atomic on final submit only. - **Single form with CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure**: Not a wizard. `display: none` removes elements from a11y tree AND form submission automatically. - **Separate domain types per GTD list**: `NextAction`, `WaitingForItem`, `SomedayMaybeItem` as independent types (not variants of a shared `Action` enum) - **Unified `actions` database table**: NextAction + WaitingFor share a single table with TEXT discriminator. Conversions are UPDATEs, not DELETE+INSERT. - **TEXT discriminator columns**: Rust enum is source of truth; DB column is just storage. No PG enum. - **"Stalled" project**: Domain term for a project with zero next actions. Soft check (`project.is_stalled()` query), not a type-level construction constraint. - **ContextName normalization**: Store without `@` prefix, display with it. `parse("@home")` and `parse("home")` produce the same value. - **Consuming InboxItem**: `clarify(self, ...)` takes ownership. Rust's borrow checker enforces that processed items leave the inbox. - **Parse, don't validate**: At form boundary too — flat form fields parsed into `ClarifyDecision` enum variants. - **Walking skeleton**: Capture -> Process to NextAction -> See it -> Complete it (the GTD walking skeleton) - **Implementation order**: Build destination list first, then its clarify path. Each step adds one `ProcessingOutcome` variant. - **Anxiety-reducing design**: UI should reduce anxiety, not create it. Inbox badge is muted, not alarming. Only stalled projects get urgent visual treatment. - **ActivePage enum**: Suggested for type-safe navigation state (non-blocking improvement for future steps) 3. Files and Code Sections: **Existing domain files reviewed (understanding current model):** - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/mod.rs` — Module exports for all domain types - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/todo_item.rs` — Current `TodoItem` enum with `Pending`/`Completed` variants, `complete(self)` and `uncomplete(self)` consuming methods, `TodoItemId(Uuid)` newtype. This is the pattern that will be extended for GTD types. - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/todo_title.rs` — `TodoTitle` newtype with `parse()` validation (non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed). Will be renamed to `ItemTitle`. - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/user.rs` — `User` struct with `UserId(Uuid)` newtype - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/email.rs` — `ValidatedEmail` with `parse()`, normalized to lowercase - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/password.rs` — `Password` with `parse()` and `hash(self)` consuming method, `PasswordHash_` for stored hashes **Project configuration files reviewed:** - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/PROJECT.md` — Updated with V1 GTD scope (Must Have: 8 user stories covering inbox, clarify, next actions, projects, waiting for, someday/maybe, weekly review, navigation) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md` — Team conventions, Definition of Done, code conventions, consensus protocol - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/startup.rs` — Router configuration with all routes; reviewed to understand current route structure before GTD routes were added **Product discovery documents reviewed:** - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` — Full product discovery: user problem, GTD methodology, domain concepts map with state machines, prioritized V1 features, 8 user stories, success criteria, data model sketch, implementation order (13 steps) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/user-stories.md` — 8 GTD user stories (US-GTD-1 through US-GTD-8) with detailed acceptance criteria, plus preserved MVP stories. Implementation order: Nav -> Inbox -> Contexts -> Next Actions -> Clarify paths interleaved -> Weekly Review -> Migration - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/glossary.md` — GTD domain types (InboxItem, NextAction, Project, Context, WaitingForItem, SomedayMaybeItem, ItemTitle, ContextName), GTD actions (clarify_as_next_action, clarify_as_project, delegate, defer, etc.), state machines - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/future-ideas.md` — Deferred features (calendar, reference files, horizons of focus, NLP, recurring items, etc.) **Task #11 implementation files reviewed (Luca's work):** - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/dashboard.rs` — Dashboard route handler with `current_page: &'static str`, `DashboardError` type, `AuthenticatedUser` extraction ```rust #[derive(Template)] #[template(path = "dashboard.html")] struct DashboardTemplate { current_page: &'static str, } pub async fn get_dashboard( AuthenticatedUser(_user_id): AuthenticatedUser, ) -> Result { let template = DashboardTemplate { current_page: "dashboard" }; Ok(Html(template.render()?)) } ``` - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/gtd_placeholders.rs` — Placeholder handlers for `/inbox`, `/next-actions`, `/projects`, `/waiting-for`, `/someday-maybe`, `/review`. Each uses `GtdPlaceholderTemplate` with `current_page`, `page_title`, `heading`, `description` fields. All require `AuthenticatedUser`. - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/dashboard.html` — Dashboard page extending base.html, includes gtd_nav.html, uses `
` for summary stats (Inbox: 0, Next Actions: 0, Stalled Projects: 0, Waiting For: 0) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/gtd_nav.html` — Shared nav partial with `
`, `
    ` of links with `aria-current="page"` conditional, quick capture form posting to `/inbox`, sign out form with `hx-boost="false"` ```html
    • Dashboard
    ``` - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/gtd_placeholder.html` — Reusable placeholder template for all GTD pages - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/mod.rs` — Updated exports including new dashboard and gtd_placeholder modules - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/tests/api/dashboard.rs` — 8 integration tests: auth redirect, 200 for authenticated, nav links present, aria-current, sign out, index redirect to /dashboard, hx-boost, quick capture form - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/tests/api/gtd_placeholders.rs` — 7 integration tests: one per GTD page (inbox, next-actions, projects, waiting-for, someday-maybe, review) plus unauthenticated redirect test for all pages 4. Errors and Fixes: - **Stale context on processing flow**: I was advocating for a wizard/HTMX-swap approach to the clarify processing flow when the team had already converged on Heydon's single-form with CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure. Heydon corrected me, explaining that `display: none` automatically removes elements from the a11y tree AND excludes them from form submission — no `aria-hidden` or `disabled` needed. I updated my position immediately. - **Migration strategy flip**: I initially recommended pending todos -> NextAction (Active, @anywhere) to "respect users' prior decisions." Luca relayed Marty's product argument that forcing users through the clarify flow teaches them GTD. I accepted this and updated my position to pending todos -> inbox_items. - **Over-constraining project invariant**: I initially suggested encoding "active project must have >= 1 NextAction" at the type level. Kent and Luca correctly pointed out this is a review-time query concern, not a construction constraint (a project legitimately has zero next actions momentarily after completing the last one). I adopted "stalled" as the domain term and `project.is_stalled()` as a query method. - **Wikipedia fetch failed**: 403 error fetching Wikipedia's GTD page. Worked around by using the hamberg.no/gtd guide and web search results instead. 5. Problem Solving: - Designed the complete GTD domain model from scratch: entities, state machines, processing pipeline, migration path - Resolved the contexts-in-Phase-1-vs-Phase-2 debate with Steve Krug using domain reasoning (contexts are what make it feel like GTD, not just a todo list with extra steps) - Identified that domain decision tree maps 1:1 to the UI flow (ClarifyDecision tree = form progressive disclosure structure) - Resolved schema strategy (unified actions table vs separate tables) with concrete argument about US-GTD-5 conversions - Established ProcessingOutcome-to-announcement mapping table as both a domain artifact and accessibility contract - Distinguished between implementation steps within V1 and separate releases (Marty's clarification) 6. All User Messages (teammate messages received): - **team-lead**: Initial spawn as Scott Wlaschin Reviewer for GTD product discovery - **luca-palmieri** (1st): Domain modeling observations — item lifecycle, projects vs actions, contexts, weekly review, schema strategy - **kent-beck**: TDD sequencing for processing pipeline, project invariant caution, walking skeleton insight, "types should emerge from tests" - **luca-palmieri** (2nd): SQLx schema strategy — Option A (ALTER TABLE) vs Option B (new tables), repository layer mapping, action_type TEXT vs PG enum question - **steve-schoger**: Visual design mapped to GTD domain views — context group headers, processing flow card design, projects, waiting for, weekly review, visual hierarchy table - **steve-krug**: Wizard beats form for processing, phased view recommendations, pushed back on exposing all 5 views in v1 - **team-lead**: Send review feedback to Marty on product discovery docs - **lea-verou**: CSS component mapping for each GTD view type — inbox, next actions grouped by context, projects with details/summary, waiting for with metadata, processing flow, weekly review - **heydon-pickering** (1st): A11y patterns — single form not wizard, nav links not tabs, heading hierarchy for context grouping, no tree views for projects, aria-live announcements - **luca-palmieri** (3rd): Agreement on domain refinements — consuming InboxItem, project invariant as query, contexts as separate entity, actions table naming, TEXT confirmed - **kent-beck** (2nd): Agreed on captured_at, collaborative TDD/domain modeling approach confirmed - **steve-schoger** (2nd): Inbox badge calm not urgent, stalled projects danger+warning treatment, completed items removal from working view, design principle "UI should reduce anxiety" - **steve-krug** (2nd): Convinced on contexts in Phase 1, updated phasing, withdrew deferral suggestion - **carson-gross**: Processing flow HTMX pattern, outcome mapping, transient state confirmed, context views, CreatedProject redirect debate, review in v1 - **luca-palmieri** (4th): Full alignment confirmed, migration update (pending->inbox from Marty discussion), schema divergence flagged - **luca-palmieri** (5th): Completion modeling options (Option A flat, Option B ActionState wrapper), converged 8-point technical position - **lea-verou** (2nd): CSS treatment details for stalled projects, processing outcomes, inbox badge alignment - **heydon-pickering** (2nd): Processing flow ALREADY RESOLVED as single form. Corrected my stale wizard assumption. display:none handles a11y automatically. Pure CSS approach wins. Middle ground unnecessary. - **steve-schoger** (3rd): Fully aligned, supporting design principle in TEAM_AGREEMENTS - **heydon-pickering** (3rd): Form field names as domain language, ProcessingOutcome-to-announcement table for glossary - **marty-cagan** (1st): Acknowledged domain review, consent noted, migration reconsideration, @anywhere debate, scope clarification - **luca-palmieri** (6th): Third completion option from glossary (separate types), domain and DB decisions are independent - **marty-cagan** (2nd): Full response — strong alignment, scope clarification (all 8 Must Have), 4 implementation questions (single vs separate tables, action enum vs types, project invariant, DoItNow) - **marty-cagan** (3rd): Phases = implementation steps within single V1, contexts are step 3 - **team-lead**: Driver rotation — Luca is Driver for Task #11 (Nav + Dashboard) - **marty-cagan** (4th): Acknowledged domain review, migration approach, quick capture rule, scope concern - **luca-palmieri** (7th): Step 1 implementation complete, awaiting review — listed all new/modified files, key decisions, test results (82 Rust + 7 Playwright) 7. Pending Tasks: - Task #11 (Implementation Step 1: Navigation + Dashboard skeleton) is awaiting 9/9 consensus. I have given CONSENT. Waiting for other reviewers. - After Task #11 consensus: Task for Step 2 (Inbox Capture, US-GTD-1) will begin — this is where heavy domain modeling starts with `InboxItem` and `ItemTitle` types. - Non-blocking improvement flagged: Migrate `current_page: &'static str` to `ActivePage` enum for type safety in a future step. 8. Current Work: I just completed my domain architect review of Luca's Task #11 implementation (Navigation + Dashboard skeleton, US-GTD-8). I read all new files (`dashboard.rs`, `gtd_placeholders.rs`, `dashboard.html`, `gtd_nav.html`, `gtd_placeholder.html`, `mod.rs`, `dashboard.rs` tests, `gtd_placeholders.rs` tests) and sent CONSENT to both Luca and the team lead. I verified: - Route naming uses GTD ubiquitous language - Quick capture form posts to `/inbox` (domain rule) - Dashboard uses `
    ` for semantic summary stats - `aria-current="page"` driven correctly - Auth protection on all GTD routes - 15 new integration tests with good coverage - Existing `/todos` routes preserved for coexistence 9. Optional Next Step: Wait for 9/9 consensus on Task #11. Once achieved, Luca will commit and push. Then the team moves to Step 2 (Inbox Capture, US-GTD-1), where my domain modeling involvement increases significantly — I'll need to guide the implementation of `InboxItem`, `ItemTitle` (renamed from `TodoTitle`), and the capture service/route. Per the team-lead's message: "We're now working on Task #11: Implementation Step 1 — Navigation + Dashboard skeleton... Please review his work as it progresses." My review is complete with CONSENT given. No further action until consensus is collected or the next task begins.
